items.title: Keeping an expensive client alive
items.scenario: You initialize an LLM client and load a dataset once, then iterate on prompts in later cells.
items.explanation: The kernel keeps the client and data in memory, so prompt tweaks re-run instantly without redoing setup.

items.title: A hidden-state surprise
items.scenario: A notebook works for you but fails for a teammate who runs it top-to-bottom.
items.explanation: You'd defined a variable in a cell you later deleted; the kernel still had it. Restart & Run All exposes the gap.

items.title: Interrupting a runaway call
items.scenario: A cell hangs waiting on a slow or stuck API request.
items.explanation: Interrupt the kernel to stop just that cell without losing the rest of your session state.

items.title: Documenting an experiment
items.scenario: You want to explain an evaluation alongside the code that produced it.
items.explanation: Markdown cells hold the narrative and conclusions; code cells hold the runnable steps and their outputs.
